description: §45 clean neg — `==` between two values of the SAME primitive type (`if (n == m)`, both `int`) SHALL NOT fire E-EQ-001. Confirms the cross-type gate does not false-fire on a well-typed same-type comparison. `notCodes` asserts E-EQ-001 stays silent.
rationale: §45: `==` between two values of the same type is the canonical valid comparison; E-EQ-001 fires only across distinct types.
